Name: Adrienne Kowalski
Bio: Adrienne Kowalski grew up in Ann Arbor and studied elementary education at the University of Michigan, where she also completed a master's degree in educational leadership. She spent four years as a classroom teacher at Riverside Elementary School, then moved into the assistant principal role there, managing daily operations and supervising a staff of more than thirty. She has been serving as acting principal at Riverside since the retirement of the longtime principal three years ago.
Personal Statement: I came to school leadership because I believe the culture of a building begins with the adults in it, and that when teachers feel genuinely supported they bring their best selves to work every day. What I hope to bring to this role is high standards for everyone, visible support, and a willingness to have honest conversations when things are not working. I am particularly passionate about early literacy, because I have seen how quickly a child who struggles to read can disengage from school, and how powerful it is to reverse that trajectory with the right instruction at the right moment.
Panel Evaluation: The full panel was impressed by the clarity and groundedness of her professional vision. She spoke with equal conviction about curriculum, culture, and operations. Her salary expectation is slightly above the posted band, but the panel believes this is worth exploring given her depth of experience and recommends advancing her to the final round.

Name: Marcus Delgado
Bio: Marcus Delgado studied child development at the University of Texas at Austin and spent his early career as a classroom teacher at Cedarwood Elementary School, where he was selected twice to lead professional development workshops on differentiated instruction. He later served as curriculum coordinator at the same school before moving on to work as an instructional coach at a neighboring school, where he provides regular coaching cycles and leads collaborative planning sessions for teachers across all grade levels.
Panel Evaluation: The panel found him to be a thoughtful and self-aware candidate whose coaching background is evident in how he listens and responds. He asks clarifying questions before answering, which several members noted as a positive sign for how he might handle difficult conversations with staff or families. The panel has no significant reservations and recommends him for the final round.

Name: Priya Nambiar
Personal Statement: I have always been drawn to the intersection of research and practice, which is why I chose to remain in my doctoral program even while holding a demanding leadership role. I believe that school leaders who read and think rigorously make better decisions than those who rely entirely on intuition, because intuition alone can lead us to replicate what has always been done without questioning it. I find the discipline of academic research energizing rather than distracting, and I believe it makes me a more deliberate and curious leader.
Panel Evaluation: She was referred by the current assistant principal, Tomas Ferreira, who spoke highly of her judgment and her ability to build trust across a diverse staff. The panel's only concern involves scheduling: she was candid about needing protected time on several evenings each week for her dissertation work. The recommendation is to have a follow-up conversation focused specifically on that question before deciding whether to advance her candidacy.
